description: Xquik TypeScript and Node.js REST SDK for X/Twitter workflows with typed methods for tweet search, user lookup, monitors, webhooks, and extractions. Not affiliated with X Corp.
rules: Use README.md first for install, API key setup, TypeScript and JavaScript examples, webhooks, and common workflows.

For remote agent access, link to https://docs.xquik.com/mcp/overview. The MCP server is separate from this REST SDK and prefers OAuth 2.1. API-key fallback is client-specific; ChatGPT custom apps require OAuth.

If Codex reports Authorization server response missing required issuer: expected https://xquik.com, do not repeat OAuth. Affected Codex releases discard the RFC 9207 iss value even though Xquik returns it. Set XQUIK_API_KEY and bearer_token_env_var = "XQUIK_API_KEY" in Codex TOML, then use https://docs.xquik.com/guides/troubleshooting#codex-oauth-issuer-validation-error and track https://github.com/openai/codex/issues/31573.
Use api.md for the generated resource map, method names, request parameters, response types, and endpoint coverage.
Use package.json and the npm package x-twitter-scraper for package metadata, exports, and supported runtimes.
Prefer X_TWITTER_SCRAPER_API_KEY environment configuration; never put API keys in source files or examples.
For TypeScript code, import the default XTwitterScraper client and use generated namespace types for request and response shapes.
Keep generated Stainless conventions intact and align feature claims with the Xquik OpenAPI and docs surface.
